fixture.description: A switch over a string-literal union omits one member and has no default, so that input falls through to an implicit `return undefined` instead of the declared boolean.

grader.rubric: PASS if the report flags that isCancellable's switch does NOT handle the "cancelled" member of OrderState and has no default, so passing "cancelled" falls through and returns undefined instead of the declared boolean — a non-exhaustive switch / missing case. It should recommend handling "cancelled" (or adding a default / exhaustiveness check). Severity IMPORTANT or DEBT. FAIL if the missing "cancelled" case is not surfaced.

expected_behavior: Bugs agent's completeness analysis flags the missing "cancelled" case
Notes the implicit undefined return for that input
Recommends handling it or adding a default
expected_not_behavior: Does NOT claim the handled cases are wrong
Does NOT require a runtime crash — returning undefined is the defect
